findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name Protocol/scheme injection in classpath resource loading
 * @description User-controlled input containing protocol prefixes (file:, jar:, url:) 
 *              flowing to ClassLoader.getResource or getResourceAsStream 
 *              where the protocol causes different resource resolution
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/protocol-scheme-injection-catalog
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe-022
 *       external/cwe-073
 */

import java
import semmle.code.java.dataflow.DataFlow

// Find getResource/getResourceAsStream calls in the camel-catalog and related classes
from MethodCall mc, string className
where
  (
    mc.getMethod().hasName("getResource") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("getResourceAsStream")
  ) and
  mc.getMethod().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("java.lang", "ClassLoader") and
  className = mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getName() and
  // Focus on catalog and resource loading classes
  (
    className.matches("%Catalog%") or
    className.matches("%Runtime%") or
    className.matches("%Resource%") or
    className.matches("%Provider%") or
    className.matches("%Version%") or
    className.matches("%Helper%")
  )
select mc, "ClassLoader.getResource() called in " + className + " - verify user input cannot control the resource path with protocol prefix injection"
findings.hypothesis: Protocol scheme injection in classpath resource loading in catalog and resource provider classes

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name Camel ResourceEndpoint header-controlled resource path 
 * @description Exchange message header (CamelXsltResourceUri/CamelLanguageResourceUri) 
 *              can override resource URI used in ResourceHelper which supports file: prefix
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/camel-resource-header-injection
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-022
 *       external/cwe/cwe-073
 */

import java

// Find all places where getHeader value flows to ResourceHelper 
from MethodCall getHeaderCall, MethodCall resolveMandatoryCall
where
  // Find exchange.getHeader or exchange.getIn().getHeader calls
  getHeaderCall.getMethod().hasName("getHeader") and
  // Find resolveMandatoryResourceAsInputStream calls
  resolveMandatoryCall.getMethod().hasName("resolveMandatoryResourceAsInputStream") and
  resolveMandatoryCall.getMethod().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("org.apache.camel.util", "ResourceHelper") and
  // Both are in the same method
  getHeaderCall.getEnclosingCallable() = resolveMandatoryCall.getEnclosingCallable()
select resolveMandatoryCall, "ResourceHelper.resolveMandatoryResourceAsInputStream called in same method as exchange.getHeader() in " + resolveMandatoryCall.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getName() + "." + resolveMandatoryCall.getEnclosingCallable().getName() + " - header value may control resource URI allowing file: prefix injection"
findings.hypothesis: Exchange header value used as resource URI passed to ResourceHelper.resolveMandatoryResourceAsInputStream allowing file: protocol injection

message.text: ResourceHelper.resolveMandatoryResourceAsInputStream called in same method as exchange.getHeader() in LanguageProducer.process - header value may control resource URI allowing file: prefix injection

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name Camel file consumer startsWith path containment without canonicalization 
 * @description GenericFileEndpoint and FileConsumer use startsWith for path containment
 *              checks without canonicalization - path traversal sequences can bypass this
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/camel-file-path-containment-startswith
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-022
 *       external/cwe/cwe-706
 */

import java

// Find startsWith calls used as security checks for file paths in file consumers 
from MethodCall startsWith, Callable enclosing
where
  startsWith.getMethod().hasName("startsWith") and
  startsWith.getMethod().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("java.lang", "String") and
  enclosing = startsWith.getEnclosingCallable() and
  (
    // GenericFileEndpoint, FileConsumer, GenericFileConsumer
    enclosing.getDeclaringType().getPackage().getName().matches("org.apache.camel.component.file%") or
    enclosing.getDeclaringType().getPackage().getName().matches("org.apache.camel.component.file.strategy%")
  ) and
  // Specifically looking for path/directory containment checks
  (
    startsWith.getQualifier().toString().toLowerCase().matches("%canonical%") or
    startsWith.getQualifier().toString().toLowerCase().matches("%absolute%") or
    startsWith.getQualifier().toString().toLowerCase().matches("%path%") or
    startsWith.getQualifier().toString().toLowerCase().matches("%file%") or
    startsWith.getAnArgument().toString().toLowerCase().matches("%dir%") or
    startsWith.getAnArgument().toString().toLowerCase().matches("%path%") or
    startsWith.getAnArgument().toString().toLowerCase().matches("%canonical%") or
    startsWith.getAnArgument().toString().toLowerCase().matches("%absolute%")
  ) and
  // No canonicalization in same method (getCanonicalPath/getCanonicalFile)
  not exists(MethodCall canon |
    (
      canon.getMethod().hasName("getCanonicalPath") or
      canon.getMethod().hasName("getCanonicalFile") or
      canon.getMethod().hasName("toRealPath")
    ) and
    canon.getEnclosingCallable() = enclosing
  )
select startsWith, "Path containment check using startsWith() in " + enclosing.getDeclaringType().getName() + "." + enclosing.getName() + " without prior path canonicalization - potentially bypassable via path traversal sequences (../, ./)"
findings.hypothesis: File consumer path containment checks using startsWith without canonicalization - bypassable via relative path traversal

message.text: Path containment check using startsWith() in FileConsumer.asGenericFile without prior path canonicalization - potentially bypassable via path traversal sequences (../, ./)

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name Archive entry getName used without path traversal check (Zip Slip)
 * @description Zip/Tar archive entry names used in file operations without checking for traversal
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/camel-zip-slip-check
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-022
 */

import java

from MethodCall getName
where
  getName.getMethod().hasName("getName") and
  (
    getName.getMethod().getDeclaringType().getASupertype*().hasQualifiedName("java.util.zip", "ZipEntry") or
    getName.getMethod().getDeclaringType().hasName("ZipArchiveEntry") or
    getName.getMethod().getDeclaringType().hasName("TarArchiveEntry")
  ) and
  // No containsCheck for ".." in same method
  not exists(MethodCall mc |
    mc.getEnclosingCallable() = getName.getEnclosingCallable() and
    mc.getMethod().hasName("contains") and
    mc.getAnArgument().(StringLiteral).getLiteral().matches("%.%")
  )
select getName, "Archive entry getName() in " + getName.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getName() + "." + getName.getEnclosingCallable().getName() + " - no path traversal ('..') check detected"
findings.hypothesis: Archive entry getName() used without any check for double-dot path traversal sequences
